commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the Anthropic-family OpenAI compatibility wrapper and tests so custom tool definitions, custom `tool_choice`, and custom `allowed_tools` entries are projected as OpenAI function payloads.

PR surface: Source +37, Tests +35. Total +72 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main and v2026.6.10 preserve custom tool shapes in the reported bridge path, and PR-head source still preserves unprojectable custom snapshots through a fallback branch. I did not run a live OpenAI failover request in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor or maintainer follow-up is needed because the PR has a blocking code defect and external real behavior proof is mock-only; this is a human-only merge blocker rather than a safe repair marker.

target.title: fix: OpenAI bridge rejects Anthropic custom tools
target.body:
Closes #97020

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users routing Anthropic-family tool payloads through an OpenAI-compatible model would hit an OpenAI 400 response when the payload included an Anthropic custom tool.

## Why This Change Was Made

The OpenAI-compatible Anthropic bridge now projects Anthropic custom tool definitions into OpenAI function tools, including pinned custom choices and allowed custom choices. The projection preserves provider metadata such as cache controls, copies `custom.input_schema` into function `parameters`, and falls back to an empty object schema when a custom tool has no input schema.

## User Impact

Tool-bearing failover/provider-bridge turns can reach OpenAI-compatible chat-completions models without leaking `type: "custom"` payloads that those models reject.

## Evidence

Real behavior proof: focused payload-shape tests now cover the bug path. Before this patch, a `type: "custom"` tool plus custom `tool_choice` stayed custom after wrapper conversion; after this patch, the wrapper emits `{ type: "function", function: { name, parameters } }` for the custom tool, named custom choice, and allowed custom choice.

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/llm/providers/stream-wrappers/anthropic-family-tool-payload-compat.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openai-tool-projection.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/llm/providers/stream-wrappers/anthropic-family-tool-payload-compat.ts src/llm/providers/stream-wrappers/anthropic-family-tool-payload-compat.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

Live OpenAI proof was attempted with `OPENAI_LIVE_TEST=1 node scripts/test-live.mjs -- src/llm/providers/stream-wrappers/anthropic-family-tool-payload-compat.live.test.ts`, but the live runner produced only its running heartbeat through 140 seconds and was stopped without using it as evidence.

AI-assisted: Yes. I used Codex to prepare and review this patch; I understand the changed provider bridge behavior and validated the focused tests above.
target.author: markoub
